failures.where_value: H1 count: 1, total headings: 68, skipped levels: True
failures.why_impact: Proper heading tags (H1-H6) act as a table of contents for crawlers and screen readers. Correct hierarchy allows search engines to understand the core topics of your page and prevents indexing confusion.
failures.how_to_fix: Use exactly one H1 per page. Don't skip heading levels (H1→H3 is bad). Use H2 for sections, H3 for subsections.

failures.why_impact: Structured data (JSON-LD) translates human-readable content into machine-readable formats. It enables rich snippets (stars, FAQs, event details) in search results, increasing search visibility and user trust.
failures.how_to_fix: Add LocalBusiness schema with name, address, phone, geo, and hours. Add FAQ schema for FAQ sections. Use Google's Rich Results Test to validate.
failures.fix_code:
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "LocalBusiness",
  "name": "#1 SEO Company | SEO.com - The Authority for All Things SEO",
  "telephone": "+1-555-555-5555",
  "address": {
    "@type": "PostalAddress",
    "streetAddress": "123 Main St",
    "addressLocality": "City",
    "addressRegion": "ST",
    "postalCode": "00000",
    "addressCountry": "US"
  },
  "geo": {
    "@type": "GeoCoordinates",
    "latitude": 0.0,
    "longitude": 0.0
  },
  "openingHoursSpecification": [
    {
      "@type": "OpeningHoursSpecification",
      "dayOfWeek": [
        "Monday",
        "Tuesday",
        "Wednesday",
        "Thursday",
        "Friday"
      ],
      "opens": "08:00",
      "closes": "17:00"
    }
  ],
  "url": "https://www.seo.com/"
}

failures.why_impact: An XML sitemap acts as a roadmap for search indexers, ensuring all page URLs are discovered and indexed, including deep-nested content that crawler bots might otherwise miss.
failures.how_to_fix: Create an XML sitemap at /sitemap.xml listing all your pages. Submit it in Google Search Console.

failures.why_impact: Broken links (404/500 errors) frustrate visitors, drain your crawl budget, and signal to search engines that the website is neglected, leading to lower search rankings.
failures.how_to_fix: Fix 3 broken links. These hurt UX and SEO.
failures.fix_code:
<!-- Fix: https://www.g2.com/products/webfx/reviews/webfx-review-7445181 returned 403 -->
<!-- Fix: https://www.g2.com/products/webfx/reviews/webfx-review-7804752 returned 403 -->
<!-- Fix: https://www.g2.com/products/webfx/reviews/webfx-review-7467300 returned 403 -->

failures.why_impact: Multiple consecutive redirects add connection latency (TTFB) and dilute link equity (SEO authority). Direct links keep connections fast and pass maximum page authority.
failures.how_to_fix: Minimize redirects. Each redirect adds latency. Direct links are better than chains.

failures.where_value: HTML breadcrumbs: False, Schema breadcrumbs: False
failures.why_impact: Breadcrumbs provide a structural navigation trail for users and search engines, showing the user's location in your site hierarchy and enhancing Google mobile search snippet displays.
failures.how_to_fix: Add breadcrumb navigation. It helps users and Google understand site structure. Add BreadcrumbList schema for rich snippets.

failures.why_impact: Leveraging browser caching stores static assets locally on user devices, eliminating redundant network calls and making repeat visits feel instantaneous.
failures.how_to_fix: Add cache-control headers and ETags to improve repeat visits.
failures.fix_code:
<IfModule mod_expires.c>
  ExpiresActive On
  ExpiresByType image/jpg "access 1 year"
  ExpiresByType image/jpeg "access 1 year"
  ExpiresByType image/gif "access 1 year"
  ExpiresByType image/png "access 1 year"
  ExpiresByType text/css "access 1 month"
  ExpiresByType text/html "access 1 month"
  ExpiresByType application/pdf "access 1 month"
  ExpiresByType text/x-javascript "access 1 month"
  ExpiresByType application/x-shockwave-flash "access 1 month"
  ExpiresByType image/x-icon "access 1 year"
  ExpiresDefault "access 1 month"
</IfModule>
<IfModule mod_headers.c>
  Header unset ETag
  FileETag None
</IfModule>

failures.where_value: Name: #1 SEO Company | SEO.com - The Authority for All Things SEO, Phone: None, Address: not found
failures.why_impact: Name, Address, and Phone (NAP) details establish localized search authority. Search engines crawl this data to confirm your physical business location for local pack matches.
failures.how_to_fix: Ensure your business Name, Address, and Phone are clearly visible on the page.

failures.why_impact: LocalBusiness schema structures business listings directly for indexers. It links business names, geo-coordinates, reviews, and hours, driving business visibility in local packs.
failures.how_to_fix: Add complete LocalBusiness schema with all required fields.

failures.why_impact: A Google Map embed confirms physical location relevance, makes navigation easy for local customers, and increases localized authority.
failures.how_to_fix: Embed a Google Map on your contact page to improve local SEO.

failures.why_impact: Declaring service areas (cities, counties, regions) clarifies business coverage to local searchers, helping match your site with searches in target cities.
failures.how_to_fix: Include service area information in your page content.

failures.where_value: No city name found in page content
failures.why_impact: Targeting specific city keywords in titles, headings, and descriptions ensures relevance for geo-modified searches (e.g., 'plumber in Dallas').
failures.how_to_fix: Include city name in your page content.

failures.why_impact: Review structured data (AggregateRating) displays star ratings in search results, increasing click-through rates (CTR) and establishing instant digital trust.
failures.how_to_fix: Add AggregateRating schema markup to your page.

failures.where_value: No Google Business Profile link found
failures.why_impact: Linking directly to your Google Business Profile page synchronizes on-page local listings with Google Maps data, reinforcing geographic relevance.
failures.how_to_fix: Add a link to your Google Business Profile on your contact page.

failures.where_value: NAP mismatch detected across pages (name differs)
failures.why_impact: Mismatched NAP details across web pages raise trust issues for search algorithms. Identical NAP details confirm listing authenticity, boosting local pack prominence.
failures.how_to_fix: Ensure NAP is identical across all pages. Use a consistent format.

failures.where_value: Keyword stuffing detected. Top words: seo (104), search (19), traffic (14), results (13), marketing (13)
failures.why_impact: Over-optimization or keyword stuffing leads to search engine penalties. Keeping target keyword density between 1-3% ensures natural reading and search relevance.
failures.how_to_fix: Reduce keyword repetition and focus on natural, valuable content.

failures.why_impact: Plain, clear reading text improves accessibility, increases time-on-site, and satisfies screen readers and voice searchers.
failures.how_to_fix: Improve readability by using shorter sentences and simpler words.

failures.why_impact: FAQ sections answer customer queries, keep users on your page longer, and capture high-value organic search real estate like 'People Also Ask' rich snippets.
failures.how_to_fix: Consider adding an FAQ section to address common user questions and improve SEO.
failures.fix_code:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [{
    "@type": "Question",
    "name": "Example Question 1",
    "acceptedAnswer": {
      "@type": "Answer",
      "text": "Example answer text here."
    }
  },{
    "@type": "Question",
    "name": "Example Question 2",
    "acceptedAnswer": {
      "@type": "Answer",
      "text": "Example answer text here."
    }
  }]
}
</script>

failures.where_value: Found 1 security headers: x-content-type-options. Missing: content-security-policy, strict-transport-security, x-frame-options, referrer-policy
failures.why_impact: Defensive HTTP headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame) protect your website against client-side scripting attacks, cross-site scripting (XSS), clickjacking, and mime-sniffing exploits.
failures.how_to_fix: Add missing security headers to protect against common web vulnerabilities
failures.fix_code:
Header always set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
Header always set X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff"
Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000"
Header always set Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin

failures.how_to_fix: Add descriptive alt text to all images
Add labels to all form inputs using <label> or aria-label
Maintain proper heading hierarchy without skipping levels
Add aria-label to interactive elements with roles
Add a skip navigation link for keyboard users
